methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
Stegra, a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Carl-Erik Lagercrantz and Harald Mix, has raised $7B+ to date. The valuation landscape in Climate & Energy comprises 21 same-sector peers, including 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms such as Ascend Elements, Redwood Materials, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Helion. However, a narrow peer median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds (fewer than 3 datapoints after filtering).

Consequently, the analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology anchored to Stegra's $7.0B cumulative raise as a valuation proxy. Applying a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier from Growth stage to the next inflection yields an implied value of $8.4B. This method assumes Stegra's capital efficiency and trajectory align with historical patterns for Growth-stage climate tech.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $7.1B to $9.7B, with a median estimate of $8.4B. The single-method foundation creates meaningful uncertainty; a broader peer-median dataset or forward cash-flow visibility would strengthen conviction. Key risks include the climate sector's capital intensity, regulatory exposure, and the absence of comparable exit precedents at this valuation tier.
